use proptest::prelude::*;

fn parse_query(input: &str) -> Result<Query, ParseError> {
    todo!()
}

const REGRESSION_CASES: &[&str] = &[
    "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = '",
    "DROP TABLE --",
    "\x00\x01\x02",
    "((((((((((",
];

#[test]
fn test_parse_regression_cases() {
    for case in REGRESSION_CASES {
        let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(|| {
            let _ = parse_query(case);
        });
        assert!(result.is_ok(), "Parser panicked on input: {:?}", case);
    }
}

proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_never_panics(input in "[a-zA-Z0-9 ]{0,100}") {
        let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(|| {
            let _ = parse_query(&input);
        });
        prop_assert!(result.is_ok());
    }
}
